## Findings
- [ ] **[BLOCK][Docs] README package table missing the new `updateapply` package**
  - **Status quo:** [README.md:33](README.md:33) lists `updatecheck` but the shipped `updatecheck/updateapply` package (the actual feature of this release, issue #24) has no row.
  - **Likely cause:** The package table was not updated when the feature was implemented.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add a row: `| `updatecheck/updateapply` | Self-update apply step: download, checksum-verify, atomic swap with rollback, re-exec |` following the existing `updatecheck` row.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` (or a direct one-line `/03-gh-go` fix, since this is a single-line addition)

- [ ] **[BLOCK][Coverage] Overall coverage regressed 85.9% → 84.6% vs v0.4.4**
  - **Status quo:** Statement coverage dropped 1.3 points since the last release, driven by the new `updateapply` package (72.5%) — see the full breakdown in the coverage report.
  - **Likely cause:** New package's defensive/error branches (nil-release guard, missing-checksum-entry guard, HTTP/I/O error paths) are untested; only the success path and the documented acceptance-criteria failure modes have tests.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add the targeted tests listed in the coverage report's findings.
  - **Handoff:** `/test-coverage` report already generated at `.github/coverage/2026-07-09T15-44-27Z.md` → `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage .github/coverage/2026-07-09T15-44-27Z.md` → `/03-gh-go`
